title: Felipe Calderón Presidency Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: García Luna's defense filed for oral arguments at the U.S. Second Circuit Court of Appeals on April 6, arguing his 38-year conviction was 'irreparably tainted' — the most consequential ongoing legal challenge to the Calderón drug war legacy. Mexico's National Registry of Disappeared reached 130,000 entries, with authorities claiming a third may still be alive — a statement families and rights groups rejected as statistical minimization of the crisis traced to post-2006 militarization. Both events were added as dated event files with full sourcing. The timeline was extended with a new post-2012 legacy era documenting García Luna's conviction (February 2023), sentencing (October 2024), Calderón's hint at political return (October 2025), and the 130,000 disappeared milestone. Political, claims, and casualties sections were updated to reflect the most current status.

title: Felipe Calderón Presidency Update — Mon Apr 6, 2026
summary: García Luna's Defense Requests Oral Argument at Second Circuit — Claims Trial 'Irreparably Tainted'.

title: Felipe Calderón Presidency Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
